name: Figma, Inc.

profile.description: Figma, Inc. develops a collaborative, browser-based platform for designing, prototyping, and building digital experiences. Products include Figma Design, FigJam, Dev Mode, Figma Slides, Figma Sites, Figma Make (AI prototyping), Figma Weave (AI media generation), and Payload CMS. Founded in 2012 by Dylan Field, IPO'd July 2025 at $33/share on the NYSE. Headquartered in San Francisco with approximately 1,886 employees.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): Figma doubled its product suite from four to eight products in 2025, shipping 200+ features including Figma Slides, Figma Sites, Figma Draw, and Figma Buzz, while growing revenue 41% YoY to $1.056B [1]. The Payload CMS acquisition extended Figma into headless content management. The July 2025 IPO at $33/share — the largest U.S. VC-backed tech IPO in four years — represented a major business model milestone. AI Deployment (1/2): Figma Make and Figma Weave deliver AI-powered prototyping and media generation, but Glassdoor reviews indicate the AI organization struggles to ship incrementally, operating in a reactive cycle driven by competitive anxiety [2]. No quantified AI revenue reported. Market Share (2/2): Figma dominates collaborative design with category-defining leadership — Q1 2026 Net Dollar Retention Rate hit 139% (highest ever) and paid customers reached 690,000, up 54% YoY [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): Full-year 2026 guidance raised to $1.422-$1.428B (35% growth at midpoint), with continued expansion into developer tools and AI monetization [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): As a design platform company with $1.64B cash and only $56M debt, R&D investment is above industry average, reflected in gross margin compression from 92% to 86% due to AI inference spending [1].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Figma's purpose — 'help teams eliminate the gap between imagination and reality' — is clear, specific, and has shaped product decisions (browser-first architecture, collaboration-first design, free tier access), but the company was founded in 2012 and only went public in 2025, lacking decades of execution to validate the mission through crisis or leadership transition [1][2]. Community Impact (1/2): Figma's Net Zero sustainability group steered Swag Store proceeds toward carbon removal credits and pre-purchased $250,000 in removals from Charm Industrial, with a net-zero-by-2040 commitment via The Climate Pledge [3]. Modest for a company of this scale. Employee Retention (0/2): Glassdoor rating of 3.7 with work-life balance score of 3.1/5.0. Multiple reviews cite 'terrible work culture,' burnout from shipping 200+ features without proportional hiring, and a deteriorating internal environment [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): Board member William McDermott purchased $5.3M in FIG stock, but the net insider trend is heavily negative at -$820M, driven by Dylan Field selling $219M and broad executive selling post-IPO [4].

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. Figma has a pristine balance sheet with D/E of 3.9% ($56M debt against $1.64B cash) scoring 1.0, and exceptional revenue growth at 46.1% YoY and 44.6% 2-year CAGR both scoring 1.0 [1]. However, GAAP profitability metrics severely penalize the score: profit margin of -123.8% and ROE of -101.7% both score 0.0, reflecting massive stock-based compensation expense. Forward P/E of 55.3 scores 0.117 (well above the 20 target), and Forward PEG of 3.27 exceeds the 3.0 floor, scoring 0.0 on the 2x-weighted growth metric [5]. Non-GAAP economics are strong — Q1 2026 delivered $52.1M non-GAAP operating income (16% margin) and $88.6M free cash flow (27% FCF margin) — but the mechanical algorithm uses reported GAAP figures.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (4/9): Figma's core design collaboration platform faces meaningful AI disruption risk as generative AI tools (Midjourney, DALL-E, and emerging AI design agents) can automate significant portions of the design workflow from wireframing to prototyping to code generation [2]. AI tools that generate UI directly from prompts could bypass traditional design tooling entirely, threatening Figma's role as the system of record. However, Figma's network effects across 690,000 paying teams, collaboration infrastructure, and design system management create defensive moats that pure AI generators lack — the platform is a system of record, not just a creation tool [1]. AI Opportunity (1/1): Figma Make (AI-powered prototyping) and Figma Weave (AI media generation) are shipping products integrated across the platform, and the raised guidance to $1.42B implies these features contribute to customer expansion and the 139% NDR [1].

infinite_game.summary: Figma under Dylan Field shows genuine purpose in democratizing design and has demonstrated courage in walking away from Adobe's $20B acquisition. However, the company is too young (founded 2012, public 2025) to have its Just Cause tested through market cycles or leadership transitions. Cultural strain from rapid feature shipping without proportional investment in people raises serious questions about sustainability, and the AI organization's reactive posture suggests finite competitive thinking.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Figma's purpose of eliminating the gap between imagination and reality has shaped meaningful product decisions — browser-based architecture enabling universal collaboration, free tier democratizing access, expansion from design into whiteboarding, presentations, and websites [1]. The cause is genuine and distinctive. However, the company has not yet been tested through economic crisis, leadership change, or multi-decade consistency — the cause is real but unproven under duress.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Field's willingness to remain independent after Adobe's $20B acquisition attempt collapsed in 2023 demonstrated conviction over windfall certainty [1]. The simultaneous expansion into eight products and heavy AI infrastructure investment at the expense of near-term margins shows long-term orientation. However, the pace of execution without commensurate investment in employee wellbeing (3.1/5.0 work-life balance score) suggests growth-at-all-costs thinking over sustainable leadership [2].

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (0/2): Multiple Glassdoor reviews describe a deteriorating culture — 'terrible work culture, Run,' 'a bad place to be at the moment,' 'Don't recommend joining right now' [2]. The company shipped 200+ features in 2025 without proportional hiring. The AI organization reportedly operates in a reactive cycle where the CEO panics over competitor announcements and engineers rush to build demos. Work-life balance score of 3.1/5.0 is among the lowest in the design/software peer set. These are signals of low psychological safety and burnout.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Figma evolved from a single design tool to an eight-product platform encompassing whiteboarding (FigJam), presentations (Slides), website publishing (Sites), developer handoff (Dev Mode), and content management (Payload CMS acquisition) [1]. These expansions show willingness to move beyond the original product. However, the pivots are incremental extensions of the design platform rather than transformative reinventions of the business model.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): Figma's competitive posture is defined by market dominance — having defeated Adobe XD and Sketch, the company holds commanding share of collaborative design [1]. The AI organization's reported tendency to react to competitor announcements with panic rather than strategic learning suggests a finite competitive mindset [2]. No evidence of management citing competitors' strengths as motivation for self-improvement.

investment_thesis: Figma is the dominant collaborative design platform with 690,000 paid customers, 139% net dollar retention (highest ever), and accelerating revenue growth to 46% YoY in Q1 2026 ($333.4M) [1]. The company raised full-year 2026 guidance to $1.422-$1.428B with non-GAAP operating income of $125-$135M and 27% free cash flow margins. With $1.64B cash, negligible debt, and expansion from design into developer tools, presentations, websites, and AI-powered prototyping, Figma has significant runway to compound its platform advantage. Category leadership with ~80% market share in collaborative design provides pricing power and deep switching costs across enterprise workflows.
thesis_continued: The primary concern is valuation disconnected from GAAP profitability. At 55.3x forward earnings and a PEG of 3.27, Figma is priced for sustained hypergrowth despite GAAP losses of -123.8% profit margin driven by stock-based compensation [5]. The stock has fallen 43% from its IPO price of $33 to $18.65, reflecting market skepticism about the path to profitable growth. Cultural strain is a material risk — Glassdoor reviews describe burnout, reactive AI strategy, and leadership that struggles to scale processes alongside product ambitions [2]. AI disruption presents an existential question: if generative AI enables non-designers to create production-quality UI from prompts, Figma's core value proposition as the design tool narrows. The company must successfully pivot from being a tool designers use to being the platform where all digital creation happens.
key_catalysts: Full-year 2026 guidance of $1.42-$1.43B (35% growth) with Q1 beat and raise suggesting further upside as new products (Sites, Buzz, Make) ramp enterprise adoption
Net Dollar Retention of 139% — highest ever — indicates strong expansion within existing accounts, with enterprise ARPU growth across the expanded eight-product suite
AI monetization through Figma Make and Weave could expand TAM from professional designers (~10M) to all knowledge workers if execution improves
key_risks: Forward PEG of 3.27 and 55.3x forward P/E at $18.65 leave zero margin of safety — any deceleration below 35% growth guidance would trigger severe multiple compression
Generative AI tools enabling non-designers to create UI directly from prompts threaten to disintermediate Figma's core design workflow, compressing the value of the collaboration layer
Cultural deterioration signaled by 3.1/5.0 work-life balance, multiple negative Glassdoor reviews, and reactive AI organization could drive talent attrition at a critical growth inflection
